---
title: "bayesian exercises"
output: html_document
date: "2025-10-16"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

```{r}
x <- seq(-5, 10, by = .1)

y <- 0.5*dnorm(x, mean = 1, sd = 2) + 0.5*dnorm(x,2,2)


plot(x,y)
```

```{r}
dat <- read.table(text = "info mean sd
info1 1 2
info2 2 2
info3 1.5 2
", header = TRUE)

densities <- apply(dat[, -1], 1, function(x) rnorm(n = 2000, mean = x[1], sd = x[2]))
colnames(densities) <- dat$info

plot(0, type = "n", xlim = c(min(densities), max(densities)), ylim = c(0, .2))
for (d in 1:ncol(densities)){
  lines(density(densities[, d]), lty = d)
}
legend("topright", legend=colnames(densities), lty=1:ncol(densities))
```

```{r}
sc<- c(-7,-5,-3,-3,1,6,7,13,15,16,20,21)
ps<-c(8,8,8,8,8,8,8,8,8,8,8,8)
diff<- sc-ps
diff
mean(diff)
sd(diff)
var(diff)
```

```{r}
p1<-1-pnorm(-8, mean(diff),sd(diff))
p2<-1-pnorm(0, mean(diff),sd(diff))
p3<-p2/p1
p1
p2
p3
```



```{r}
mydata <- data.frame(x = diff)
ggplot(mydata, aes(x)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), binwidth = 0.5,
                 fill = "cornsilk", color = "black") +
  stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(mean = mean(diff), sd = sd(diff)), color = "maroon")
```
